title: UX Researcher — AI & Agent Products
companyDescription: Synthwave AI is building autonomous research and analysis agents for enterprise customers. Seed-stage (raised $12M from top-tier VCs). Team of 35.
description:
Synthwave AI builds autonomous research agents that help companies gather market intelligence, analyze competitors, and synthesize industry trends. Our agents can run multi-step research workflows — searching databases, analyzing documents, interviewing simulated personas — and produce comprehensive reports.

We need a UX researcher who can study how humans interact with autonomous research agents. How do users trust the agent's findings? How do they verify results? How do they intervene when the agent goes off track? Your research will shape the design of our agent's transparency features, its explanation model, and its error recovery patterns.

This is a rare opportunity to define research methods for a new category: research on AI agents themselves. You'll develop new evaluation frameworks, run experiments on agent-human collaboration, and provide the data that drives our design decisions.
responsibilities: Design and execute research studies on how users interact with autonomous AI agents, including usability tests, interviews, and diary studies
Develop evaluation frameworks for agent behavior — measuring trust, transparency, and user control
Analyze conversation logs to identify patterns in agent failures, user corrections, and trust-building moments
Run A/B tests on different agent explanations, confidence indicators, and error recovery strategies
Conduct competitive research on AI agent products and their UX approaches
Present research findings to the product and design team, with actionable recommendations
Build a research repository and knowledge base for ongoing agent UX insights
requirements: 3+ years of UX research experience, preferably in AI/ML products
Strong mixed-methods background — comfortable with both qualitative and quantitative research
Experience designing and analyzing conversations between humans and AI systems
Ability to design studies that evaluate non-deterministic, autonomous systems
Proficiency in research tools (UserTesting, Dovetail, Lookback, etc.)
Strong analytical skills — you can turn qualitative insights into quantitative signals
Excellent written communication — research reports that people actually read
niceTo-have: Experience researching AI agents or autonomous systems
Background in human-computer interaction or cognitive science
Statistical analysis skills (R, Python, or similar)
Experience with AI/ML model evaluation or red-teaming
Publications in HCI or UX research venues
